steps.title: Identify what both texts reveal about bison and elk steroid profiles
steps.content: Text 1 explicitly states that bison and elk samples had similar levels of beta-sitosterol and stigmastanol. Text 2 confirms this: steroidal profiles alone cannot distinguish bison from elk, confirming that their profiles are fundamentally similar.

steps.title: Apply the similarity to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice A directly captures what both texts demonstrate: bison and elk profiles contain similar concentrations of steroids. Choices B, C, and D either contradict this similarity or require information neither text provides. That matches choice A — A is correct.
